reforms.description: The new Law on Universities, adopted in 2002, transforms tertiary institutions into non-governmental organisations and introduces tuition fees for part-time students, aiming to increase private funding for higher education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing private funding for higher education can enhance the quality and accessibility of education, leading to a more skilled workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was adopted and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly alters the structure and funding of higher education institutions.
reforms.source_quote: The new Law on Universities, adopted in 2002, transforms tertiary institutions into non-governmental organisations, makes tuition fees for 'external' (part-time) students applicable...

reforms.description: A comprehensive tax reform launched in 2004 cuts the income tax rate on both wages and profits to 19%, eliminates taxation of dividends, and enhances incentives for entrepreneurship and work, thereby improving the transparency of the tax system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Lowering tax rates and eliminating dividend taxes incentivizes business creation and self-employment, fostering economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax reform has been successfully implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant overhaul of the tax system aimed at enhancing economic activity.
reforms.source_quote: A comprehensive tax reform has been launched to enhance incentives for entrepreneurship and work, increasing the transparency of the tax system, and reducing distortions from exemptions and double taxation.

reforms.description: In 2003, the Slovak Republic initiated a major reform to establish a medium-term budget framework, aiming to project government revenues and expenditures over a three-year period, thereby enhancing fiscal transparency and planning.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improved fiscal planning and transparency can lead to better allocation of resources and more effective public investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The framework has been established and is currently in use.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a medium-term budget framework represents a significant change in fiscal policy.
reforms.source_quote: The government then announced a major initiative in 2003, promising to submit a three-year budget framework to Parliament together with the 2005 budget act.

reforms.description: The government introduced a 'micro loans' scheme and a 'seed capital' facility for new ventures, funded from EU resources, to improve access to credit for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs).
reforms.component_name: Micro Loans and Seed Capital Facility
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to credit for SMEs can stimulate entrepreneurship and innovation, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The schemes have been introduced and are operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the reform is more focused and does not represent a system-wide change.
reforms.source_quote: The government also introduced a 'micro loans' scheme and a 'seed capital' facility for new ventures, funded from EU resources.
